items.title: GEO auto-apply carries display_name into the new draft version — verified by Layer 1 test
items.precondition: No drivable UI surface: applyGeoToPage() is an internal step of the SEO/GEO generator's auto-apply flow, triggered only after a real AI-generated opening_content_recommendation. Reproducing it live requires an authenticated admin session plus a real generation run, which this session does not drive (established constraint: no admin-auth browser automation).
items.expected: insertData.display_name passed to PageModel.createPageVersion equals the source page's display_name (page.display_name || null), mirroring createDraft's already-verified behavior.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/service.seo-generation.test.ts — 'pages: non-empty opening_content_recommendation creates a NEW page version row' now asserts versionArgs.insertData.display_name === 'Teeth Cleaning Services' against a mocked source page carrying that label. Full suite: 18 files / 158 tests passed. npx tsc --noEmit clean. npm run check:conventions --strict: 0 backend structural violations.
items.notes: Layer 1 (automated test) is the real proof here; this item exists only to satisfy the acceptance-artifact convention for -i with an honest N/A rather than fabricated click-steps.
